title: marchantiopsida
intro: The Marchantiopsida rank is a division of non-vascular plants commonly known as liverworts, characterized by their flat, leaf-like structures and simple reproductive methods. Marchantiopsida is the name given to this group of plants, which includes over 700 species. Other divisions of plants include the Commelinidae, which includes flowering plants such as bamboo and orchids, and the Rosidae and Asteridae, which comprise a diverse range of flowering plants. The Marchantiopsida division is notable for its unique characteristics and adaptations, allowing it to thrive in a wide range of environments.

subclasses.subclass_desc: Marchantiidae is a subclass of liverworts, a group of small, non-vascular plants that are primarily found in moist environments.
